items.title: Introducing our Science Blog — Anthropic
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has made several significant announcements in early 2026. Most notably, on April 7, 2026, Anthropic unveiled Claude Mythos Preview — a new general-purpose language model with exceptional capability in computer security tasks. In response to its security prowess, Anthropic simultaneously launched Project Glasswing, an initiative to leverage Mythos Preview to help secure critical software infrastructure. Anthropic also introduced Managed Agents in public beta, a fully managed agent harne

items.title: Claude Platform - Claude API Docs (Release Notes)
items.author: platform.claude.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. Most notably, Claude Managed Agents launched in public beta — a fully managed agent harness for running Claude autonomously with secure sandboxing, built-in tools, and server-sent event streaming (requires the managed-agents-2026-04-01 beta header). The ant CLI was also introduced as a command-line client for the Claude API, enabling faster iteration and native Claude Code integration. On the model front, Claude Sonnet 4.6 and

items.title: Claude Mythos: Anthropic's Next Major Model Is Already Real
items.author: www.maverickai.it
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a major 2026 so far with the launch of Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 in February 2026. Claude Opus 4.6 features a 1-million-token context window, significantly stronger coding and planning capabilities, improved financial analysis, and an extended 14.5-hour task completion time horizon. Claude Sonnet 4.6 represents a full upgrade across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, and knowledge work, also offering a 1M token context window in beta. Anth

items.title: Anthropic faces user backlash over Claude performance issues
items.author: fortune.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's (@AnthropicAI) most recent Twitter/X announcements in April 2026 center on several major developments. On April 14, 2026, the Long-Term Benefit Trust appointed Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan to Anthropic's Board of Directors, signaling deeper focus on safety and healthcare-grade AI deployment in regulated sectors. Also announced was new research from the Anthropic Fellows program in which Claude Opus 4.6 is being used to automate parts of alignment research via weak-to-strong supervisio
